license: U.S. Government work product, public domain

tossup_variants.question: Niels Bohr developed a model of the atom that explained why different elements burn in different colors. In this model, the light emitted by burning is due to changes in what subatomic particle?

bonus_variants.question: Identify all of the following three properties of matter that are extensive: 1) Mass; 2) Volume; 3) Density.

tossup_variants.question: From time to time, arthropods must shed their exoskeletons to continue growing. What is the term for this process?

bonus_variants.question: During prophase I, what process results in the exchange of DNA between homologous chromosomes?
bonus_variants.answer: CROSSING OVER (ACCEPT: SYNAPSIS; do not accept: RECOMBINATION)

tossup_variants.question: What is the term for double- replacement reactions between acids and bases?

bonus_variants.question: In the combustion of ethane, how many water molecules are produced for every oxygen molecule used?

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following is NOT found in an animal cell? W) Mitochondrion X) Cytoskeleton Y) Endoplasmic reticulum Z) Cell wall

bonus_variants.question: In a multicellular organism, cells in different tissues have very different functions. Identify all of the following three features that are typically different between two such cells: 1) DNA sequence; 2) Transcriptomic profile; 3) Proteomic profile.

tossup_variants.question: While Newton's first law deals with linear motion, spinning objects also have a tendency to remain spinning in the absence of external torques . This is due to the conservation of what quantity?
tossup_variants.answer: ANGULAR MOMENTUM (ACCEPT: ROTATIONAL MOMENTUM, MOMENT OF MOMENTUM)

bonus_variants.question: In most organic compounds, how many covalent bonds does nitrogen make to other atoms?

tossup_variants.question: What is the term for sequence alterations within DNA that may result from errors in replication, exposure to radiation or chemicals, or viral infection?

bonus_variants.question: What is the general term for viruses that infect prokaryotes ?

tossup_variants.question: What is the general term for hypersensitivity reactions of the immune system towards harmless environmental stimuli?

bonus_variants.question: Which of the following types of plants is considered non-vascular? W) Fern X) Moss Y) Oak Z) Conifer

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following elements is the most electronegative? W) Sodium X) Boron Y) Carbon Z) Rubidium

bonus_variants.question: Identify all of the following three compounds that would burn green in a flame test: 1) Barium chloride; 2) Potassium chloride; 3) Lithium chloride.

tossup_variants.question: Pepper the cat that likes to hunt. He brings home the skull of an unidentified animal with very sharp and robust canines. What did this unknown animal most likely eat? W) Meat X) Detritus Y) Grasses Z) Berries

bonus_variants.question: What part of the eye contains photoreceptors and is responsible for detecting light?

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following changes would be most likely to increase the terminal velocity of a falling object? W) Increasing the size of the object without changing its mass X) Decreasing the acceleration of gravity Y) Adding mass to the object without changing its size Z) Increasing the viscosity of the atmosphere
tossup_variants.answer_text: Adding mass to the object without changing its size

bonus_variants.question: Magnesium hydroxide can be taken to neutralize the acidity in your stomach. What is the chemical formula of the primary salt this reaction will produce in your stomach?

tossup_variants.question: What is the most abundant molecule found in the cytosol of cells?

bonus_variants.question: What biopolymer is the primary constituent of the cell wall of fungi?

tossup_variants.question: What force provides the centripetal acceleration needed to keep a satellite in circular orbit about a planet?

bonus_variants.question: What is the term for a substance that speeds up a chemical reaction without being consumed?

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following events is least likely to be associated with initiation of seed germination? W) Seed coat abrasion X) Water absorption Y) Respiratory restriction Z) Light and temperature modulation

bonus_variants.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the innate immune system? W) Neutrophil X) Basophil Y) Natural killer cell Z) Antibody

tossup_variants.question: What SI unit measures the same quantity as the US measure of weight, the pound? W) Newton X) Kilogram Y) Joule Z) Candela

bonus_variants.question: Consider the reaction of 3 moles of phosphoric acid with 2 moles of sodium hydroxide. To the nearest gram, how much water will be produced by this reaction?

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following types of molecules is not generally composed of a long chain of monomers? W) Fatty acid X) Protein Y) DNA Z) Starch

bonus_variants.question: Consider a cross between two plants that are heterozygous for a certain monogenic trait. What percentage of the offspring of this cross will also be heterozygous for this trait?

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following molecules has a pyramidal geometry? W) Methane X) Water Y) Carbon dioxide Z) Ammonia

bonus_variants.question: Rank the following three regions of the electromagnetic spectrum in terms of increasing frequency: 1) Infrared; 2) Radio; 3) Gamma.

tossup_variants.question: The tracheal system in insects includes many small external openings on the sides of their bodies. What function does this system serve? W) Respiration X) Reproduction Y) Digestion Z) Locomotion

bonus_variants.question: After a forest fire, annual plants will begin growing in the burnt area fairly quickly. Which of the following best explains why? W) Forest fires do not usually kill all of the local trees X) Forest fires expose bare rock, which is a good substrate for annual plant growth Y) As a climax community, forests cannot be destroyed Z) Forest fires do not destroy the topsoil, which supports plant growth
bonus_variants.answer_text: Forest fires do not destroy the topsoil, which supports plant growth

tossup_variants.question: For an incandescent lightbulb, if the temperature of the coil were doubled, by what value would the peak frequency of emission be multiplied?

bonus_variants.question: A certain engine operates from a hot reservoir with a temperature of 500 kelvins and a cold sink with a temperature of 300 kelvins. As a percentage, what is the Carnot efficiency of this engine?

tossup_variants.question: What general series of reactions do cells use to convert the energy in sugar into ATP?

bonus_variants.question: What type of biome is characterized by sparse vegetation and less than 25 centimeters of rainfall a year?

tossup_variants.question: What is the term for matter that is made of only one kind of atom?

bonus_variants.question: What is the SI derived unit, equal to one joule per coulomb, that is used to quantify electric potential?

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following structures is too small to be seen reliably with a basic light microscope with a 400-times objective? W) Frog egg X) Viral capsid Y) Chloroplast Z) Bacterial cell

bonus_variants.question: Place the following three events in order from earliest to latest in the process of generating protein from a DNA sequence in eukaryotes: 1) Translocation of mRNA to the cytoplasm; 2) Transcription; 3) Translation.

tossup_variants.question: Sarah launches a projectile over flat ground at 10 meters per second at an angle of 15 degrees. If Jake wants to launch a projectile the same horizontal distance, which of the following launch parameters would be appropriate? W) 5 meters per second at 45 degrees X) 5 meters per second at 75 degrees Y) 10 meters per second at 45 degrees Z) 10 meters per second at 75 degrees

bonus_variants.question: A circuit consists of an 18-volt battery wired in series with a 4-ohm resistor. In watts, how much power is dissipated by the resistor?

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following has the lowest energy wavelength? W) Radio waves X) Blue light Y) Infrared light Z) Gamma rays

bonus_variants.question: When you hang a box from a spring with a spring constant of 30 newtons per meter, the spring is extended by 1.3 meters. To the nearest kilogram, what is the mass of the box?

tossup_variants.question: What human organ system is responsible for transporting blood throughout the body?

bonus_variants.question: What is the term for the genetic interaction in which two alleles are both equally expressed, resulting in a phenotype such as AB blood type that has properties of both traits?

tossup_variants.question: Laser A has a wavelength of 400 nanometers, and laser B has a wavelength of 800 nanometers. Which of the following is NOT true? W) Light from laser A is blue, and light from laser B is near infrared X) Photons from laser A have half the energy of those from laser B Y) Light from laser A has twice the frequency of laser B Z) Light from lasers A and B travel at the same speed in vacuum
tossup_variants.answer_text: Photons from laser A have half the energy of those from laser B

bonus_variants.question: A 1200-kilogram car is parked on a 30-degree incline. If the car remains parked for eight hours, how much work, in joules, is done by the brakes to keep it from rolling down the incline?

tossup_variants.question: The information that encodes a protein sequence is located in what group of macromolecules, of which DNA and RNA are examples?

bonus_variants.question: What structure connects the middle ear to the throat?
bonus_variants.answer: EUSTACHIAN TUBE (ACCEPT: AUDITORY TUBE, PHARYNGOTYMPANIC TUBE)

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following pairs is NOT a correct matching of an organism with its external symmetry classification? W) Heliozoan and spherical X) Crocodile and bilateral Y) Starfish and radial Z) Lobster and biradial

bonus_variants.question: Place the following three locations in chronological order that a breath of air will pass through during human inhalation: 1) Larynx ; 2) Pharynx ; 3) Bronchi .

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following is the shortest amount of time? W) 5 microseconds X) 1 nanosecond Y) 2000 picoseconds Z) 15 milliseconds

bonus_variants.question: How much power, in watts, is used by a laptop computer with a 12-volt battery and a current draw of 5 amps?

tossup_variants.question: Penicillin is able to inhibit bacterial growth without harming human cells because it targets what bacterial structure that animal cells lack?

bonus_variants.question: In the human heart, what other chamber contracts at the same time as the left ventricle?

tossup_variants.question: What type of heat transfer process occurs when heat flows from a hot object to a cold object that it is directly touching?

bonus_variants.question: Identify all of the following three statements that are true regarding astronauts in low Earth orbit: 1) Their mass is nearly zero; 2) Their weight is nearly zero; 3) They experience no gravitational force from the Earth.

tossup_variants.question: When two different waves end up in the same location, they undergo what phenomenon that can either be constructive or destructive?

bonus_variants.question: A 3-volt battery is connected to a resistor with resistance of six ohms. In watts, what is the power dissipated by the resistor?

tossup_variants.question: In female mammals, the initiation of meiosis only occurs in what organ?

bonus_variants.question: During birth, oxytocin results in uterine contractions, which eventually results in the production of more oxytocin. Of what type of regulation is this an example?

tossup_variants.question: What is the general term for a process that results in a chemical change?

bonus_variants.question: The net torque on an object describes the rate of change of what quantity over time?

tossup_variants.question: What series of chemical reactions are performed by plants to synthesize sugars from water and carbon dioxide?

bonus_variants.question: Identify all of the following three trophic levels that directly contribute energy flow to detritivores : 1) Primary producers; 2) Primary consumers; 3) Secondary consumers.

tossup_variants.question: You put two solutions with different concentrations of salt in a container, separated by a semi-permeable membrane. Later, you observe that the water level on one side of the container is higher than the other. What effect is responsible for this?

bonus_variants.question: When only considering the ABO blood group, identify all of the following three types of blood that a person with blood type O could safely receive: 1) A; 2) B; 3) AB.

tossup_variants.question: What is the term for the electric configuration of an object composed of a positive charge separated by a distance to a negative charge of equal magnitude?

bonus_variants.question: The carboxyl functional group is MOST associated with which of the following classes of molecules? W) Alcohols X) Amines Y) Ketones Z) Organic acids

tossup_variants.question: Some animals, such as frogs and butterflies, undergo a process in which their larval form makes a large transformation in body shape as they become adults. What is the term for this process?

bonus_variants.question: What is the largest organ in the human integumentary system?

tossup_variants.question: What is the term for a device that converts radiant energy directly into electrical energy?

bonus_variants.question: What solid-state electrical component only allows current to flow in one direction?

tossup_variants.question: What layer of the eye is responsible for absorbing light and sending that information to the optic nerve?

bonus_variants.question: What retroviral enzyme catalyzes the formation of DNA from a viral RNA template?

tossup_variants.question: What type of simple machine is a knife?

bonus_variants.question: A torque applied by the brakes on a bicycle wheel that was initially spinning at 6 radians per second causes it to spin to a halt in a half of a second. To the nearest revolution per second squared, what was the magnitude of the angular acceleration of the wheel in this time?

tossup_variants.question: While most complexes in the cell are made of proteins, ribosomes are mostly composed of protein and what other macromolecule?
tossup_variants.answer: NUCLEIC ACIDS (ACCEPT: RNA, RIBONUCLEIC ACIDS; DO NOT ACCEPT: DNA)

bonus_variants.question: Amylase catalyzes the digestion of starch into sugars. The presence of high amounts of amylase in the blood is likely to be associated with dysfunction of which of the following organs? W) Pancreas X) Kidney Y) Brain Z) Heart

tossup_variants.question: Mary has a scale, which she decides to calibrate using a one- kilogram mass. Which of the following sets of measurements in kilograms reflect the most precise calibration? W) 2.1, 2.1, 2.0 X) 0.6, 1.0, 1.4 Y) 1.0, 0.8, 0.6 Z) 0.7, 1.3, 1.0

bonus_variants.question: Alison has two vertical pistons with areas of 10 meters squared and 300 meters squared, respectively, that are connected via a liquid-filled tube. If she wants to exert an upward force of 4,500 newtons on the larger piston, then, in newtons, how much force should she exert downward on the smaller piston?

tossup_variants.question: Electrical utility poles often have large cylindrical devices attached that convert high voltage currents into lower voltages suitable for residential use. What is the term for these devices?

bonus_variants.question: Identify all of the following three waves that are purely transverse waves: 1) Water waves; 2) Light waves; 3) Pressure waves.

tossup_variants.question: What state function has a low value for crystalline solids and a high value for gases, and quantifies the number of different ways a system can be arranged at a given energy?

bonus_variants.question: In Thomson’s experiments with cathode ray tubes, he noted that the presence of a magnet caused the cathode rays to bend. What did he conclude from this observation? W) Cathode rays are made of charged particles X) Cathode rays are made of electrons Y) Cathode rays are massless Z) Cathode rays are less massive than the hydrogen atom
bonus_variants.answer_text: Cathode rays are made of charged particles

tossup_variants.question: Which of the following best portrays an example of evolution by natural selection that has been directly observed in modern times? W) Seedlessness in watermelons X) Speciation of great apes Y) Domestication of cattle Z) Antibiotic resistance in bacteria

bonus_variants.question: The discovery of iron-containing fragments in the skulls of birds suggests that they use what phenomenon to navigate during migrations?
bonus_variants.answer: MAGNETISM (ACCEPT: EARTH'S MAGNETIC FIELD, MAGNETIC FIELD)

tossup_variants.question: Most nerves in mammals are covered with a sheath of myelin . Which of the following is the main function of this layer? W) Sending larger impulses X) Increasing structural stability Y) Speeding up transmission Z) Protection from pathogens

bonus_variants.question: Most bacteria undergo a form of asexual reproduction in which the cells divide into two. What is the term for this type of reproduction?
bonus_variants.answer: FISSION (ACCEPT: BINARY FISSION; DO NOT ACCEPT: MITOSIS)

bonus_variants.question: During what phase of meiosis does crossing over occur?
bonus_variants.answer: PROPHASE I (ACCEPT: PACHYTENE; DO NOT ACCEPT: PROPHASE)

bonus_variants.question: The volume of a balloon is expanded from one liter to five liters. The temperature is held constant at 20 degrees Celsius and the initial pressure was 10 atmospheres. In atmospheres, what is the new pressure?

tossup_variants.question: The atomic number of molybdenum is 42. What does this number indicate? W) Every atom has 42 total protons & neutrons in the nucleus X) Every atom has a mass of 42 a.m.u. Y) Every atom has 42 protons in the nucleus Z) Every atom will form bonds that measure 42 degrees
tossup_variants.answer_text: Every atom has 42 protons in the nucleus
